title: galium angustifolium subsp. gabrielense
intro: Galium angustifolium subsp. gabrielense, also known as Gabriel's beak or Gabriel's spurflower, is a subspecies of the Galium angustifolium plant, which is a flowering perennial found in North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Galium angustifolium subsp. gabrielense include relief from digestive issues, such as diarrhea and nausea, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
